message:
chore(beads): sync issue tracker state

Update local issue tracker reflecting blocking pool deduplication,
pool warmup timeout, weighted strategy sync, and idempotency inline TTL.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"asupersync-1qfd0.18","title":"[audit][blocking-pool] Avoid unchecked idle-timeout subtraction in worker park path","description":"The blocking pool worker loop computes remaining idle park time with a direct idle_timeout minus elapsed subtraction after a preceding branch check. Clippy flags this as unchecked time subtraction, and a saturating or checked subtraction is clearer and more robust against refactors or timing edge cases. Patch the worker park path in src/runtime/blocking_pool.rs to use a non-panicking subtraction and add focused regression coverage if needed.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","assignee":"SilentSpring","created_at":"2026-03-08T22:38:13.600586532Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-08T22:43:59.629469523Z","closed_at":"2026-03-08T22:43:58.499840584Z","close_reason":"Completed: blocking_pool now uses saturating idle-timeout subtraction; rch lib clippy passed; rch blocking_pool lib tests passed (35).","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["audit","blocking-pool","runtime"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"asupersync-1qfd0.18","depends_on_id":"asupersync-1qfd0","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-08T22:38:13.600586532Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"asupersync-1qfd0.19","title":"[audit][dns-cache] Make capacity-eviction tie-break deterministic","description":"DnsCache capacity eviction falls back to find_oldest_key() over a HashMap. When multiple entries share the same inserted_at Instant, min_by_key(inserted_at) inherits randomized hash iteration order, so the victim key can vary across runs. Patch src/net/dns/cache.rs to break ties deterministically (preserving oldest-first semantics) and add focused regression coverage for equal-timestamp eviction behavior.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","assignee":"DarkPrairie","created_at":"2026-03-08T22:40:45.398238664Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-08T22:51:21.109218770Z","closed_at":"2026-03-08T22:51:21.109193573Z","close_reason":"Made DNS cache capacity eviction deterministic for equal inserted_at ties; added focused regression coverage; rch cargo check --all-targets and targeted test passed; workspace clippy frontier is unrelated in transport tests.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["audit","cache","determinism","dns"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"asupersync-1qfd0.19","depends_on_id":"asupersync-1qfd0","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-08T22:40:45.398238664Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}

- Standardize failure summaries + links to artifacts.
- Create repro manifests with seeds, env, and steps.
- Integrate replay tooling (trace replay / lab runtime).
- Document a fast local repro workflow.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"GoldBasin","created_at":"2026-02-03T15:52:06.088991406Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-03T18:26:53.229518303Z","closed_at":"2026-02-03T18:26:53.229448744Z","close_reason":"Failure triage + repro pipeline complete:

src/test_logging.rs:
- Added 3 fields to ReproManifest: env_snapshot, phases_executed, failure_reason
- Added capture_test_env() for reproducible env snapshots
- Added builder methods: with_env_snapshot(), with_phases(), with_failure_reason()
- Added phases_executed() and repro_manifest() on TestHarness
- Integrated auto-manifest generation in TestHarness::finish() on failure
- 6 new unit tests covering all new functionality

tests/replay_debugging.rs:
- 4 new integration tests demonstrating full capture→manifest→replay workflow
- End-to-end test: lab trace capture, manifest persistence, reload, deterministic replay
- Manifest field preservation roundtrip, seed-based replay verification
- Trace file persistence + replayer verification

All tests pass.  {"id":"asupersync-3or","title":"[EPIC-TOKIO] gRPC Framework (tonic equivalent)","description":"# gRPC Framework

## Overview
Full gRPC implementation with all streaming patterns and code generation.

## Streaming Patterns

### 1. Unary
- Request -> Response
- Simple RPC

### 2. Server Streaming
- Request -> Stream<Response>
- Server sends multiple responses

### 3. Client Streaming
- Stream<Request> -> Response
- Client sends multiple requests

### 4. Bidirectional Streaming
- Stream<Request> -> Stream<Response>
- Full duplex streaming

## Core Types

### Request<T>
- Metadata (headers)
- Message body
- Extensions

### Response<T>
- Metadata
- Message body
- Trailers

### Streaming<T>
- Async stream of messages
- Backpressure via flow control

### Status
- gRPC status codes
- Error details
- Rich error model support

## Server

### Service Definition
```rust
#[async_trait]
pub trait MyService: Send + Sync + 'static {
    async fn my_method(&self, request: Request<MyRequest>) 
        -> Result<Response<MyResponse>, Status>;
}
```

### Server Builder
- Add services
- Configure limits
- TLS setup
- Interceptors

## Client

### Client Stub
- Generated from proto
- Connection management
- Retry policies

### Channel
- Connection to server
- Load balancing
- Health checking

## Code Generation
- Build script integration
- Compile .proto to Rust
- Both client and server stubs

## Cancel-Safety
- Streams: cancel closes stream
- Unary: cancel aborts in-flight
- Deadlines: gRPC deadline -> budget

## Interceptors
- Request/response modification
- Authentication
- Logging
- Metrics

## Health Checking
- gRPC health protocol
- Service health status

## Reflection
- Server reflection for debugging

 {"id":"asupersync-3ouqo","title":"[SPORK][App][Crash] Crashpack Hook: emit minimal repro on failure","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"SapphireCreek","created_at":"2026-02-06T05:43:30.905076970Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-08T08:13:19.164987402Z","closed_at":"2026-02-08T08:13:19.164922351Z","close_reason":"Completed: crashpack hook now emits deterministic crashpack with divergent-prefix attachment for failing app scenarios; targeted crashpack integration tests pass.","external_ref":"bd-3mqxu","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["app","crash","replay","spork"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"asupersync-3ouqo","depends_on_id":"asupersync-17hxp","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T18:30:27Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"asupersync-3ouqo","depends_on_id":"asupersync-1fvt3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T18:30:27Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"asupersync-3ouqo","depends_on_id":"asupersync-2ef8r","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T18:30:27Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"asupersync-3ouqo","depends_on_id":"asupersync-jqgau","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-13T18:30:27Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":1168,"issue_id":"asupersync-3ouqo","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"## Goal

When an app run fails (panic, invariant violation, etc.), produce the minimal deterministic artifacts required to reproduce.

This task is specifically about *hooking* the existing crashpack pipeline into the application lifecycle.

## Deliverable

- Define where crashpack building is initiated:
  - app root
  - supervisor decision points
  - trace recorder

- Ensure crashpack contents are deterministic:
  - stable ordering
  - canonical trace fingerprint

## Acceptance

- Failing app scenario produces a crashpack manifest and minimal divergent prefix.
- Replay uses crashpack to deterministically reproduce the failure.

 {"id":"asupersync-3p1ty","title":"Implement race_all combinator (N-way race with loser draining)","description":"# race_all (N-way Race with Loser Draining)

## Purpose
Generalize `race(a,b)` to `race_all(futures)`:
- first terminal outcome wins
- every loser is cancelled and drained to terminal

This is essential for timeouts, hedges, speculative execution, and quorum-style patterns.

## Semantics
1. spawn all participants in a subregion
2. wait for the first terminal completion
3. request cancellation on every loser
4. **drain** every loser by awaiting completion
5. return winner outcome

## Acceptance Criteria
- Winner is returned.
- Every spawned participant reaches a terminal state before `race_all` returns.
- Loser finalizers run.
- Loser obligations are resolved.

## Testing
- E2E: include a loser holding a permit/guard; verify it is released due to drain.
